workiqQueriesRun: What email threads did I send or receive most frequently in the past 7 days? List subject patterns, sender/recipient groups, and approximate time spent.
Are there recurring email types I write regularly -- like status updates, approvals, scheduling requests, or data requests? Show examples from the past week.
Which emails required the most back-and-forth (most replies in a thread) this past week, and what was the topic?
What recurring meetings did I attend this past week? For each, what was the typical agenda type and who attends?
How much total time did I spend in meetings this past week, broken down by meeting type (1:1, team sync, external, etc.)?
Which meeting types happen every week at roughly the same time with the same people?
What Teams channels or chats am I most active in over the past 7 days? What topics come up repeatedly?
Are there questions I get asked repeatedly in Teams chats -- things people regularly come to me for?
What types of information do I most frequently share or look up in Teams conversations this week?
What types of documents did I create, edit, or review most often this past week? Any recurring doc types like reports, specs, or decks?
Are there documents I update on a regular cadence (weekly/monthly reports, trackers, dashboards)?
What SharePoint sites or OneDrive folders do I access most frequently?
Across email, meetings, and Teams, what topics or projects consumed the most of my time this week?
Are there workflows that seem to repeat -- where I do the same sequence of actions (e.g., get email -> schedule meeting -> create doc)?
What tasks do multiple people on my team seem to do independently that could be standardized?

signals.title: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: ~198 emails total in search window. Activity heavily skewed toward system-generated Azure DevOps notifications. Recurring prefixes: Task, Bug, Requirement, Feature, Epic. Mentions of Evals, IcM, Quality evaluation, RAI Evaluation appearing dozens of times.

signals.title: CAPE and Evals Strategy Discussion Threads
signals.rawExcerpt: High-signal threads: Re: CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive, Re: Customer donated evals added Playbook, Fw: Announcing EvalCon. Longer emails with summaries and action items. High impact, lower frequency.

signals.title: Security and Compliance Alert Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject pattern: Action Required File Blocked appears multiple times for different files. Sender: Office365/CISO/Purview. Usually: quick open, check file name, decide whether action is needed.

signals.title: Internal Broadcast and All-Hands Digest Consumption
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject patterns: BIC All Hands Pre-event reminders, Viva Engage digests, Copilot Champs Weekly Digest posts. Senders: BIC Communications, Viva Engage, Employee Advocacy. Skim depending on relevance.

signals.title: Collaboration Workspace Invitation Sending
signals.rawExcerpt: Only sent emails clearly surfaced this week: automated Microsoft Loop workspace collaboration invitations. Subject: You have been looped in! sent to collaborators to onboard them to Skilluminator Workspace.

signals.title: Partner and Customer AI Agent Adoption Follow-Up Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread: INVITATION Operationalizing AI Agents - most back-and-forth this week. Multiple nested replies spanning several days. Topics: EY maturity and challenges around Copilot agent adoption, guidance on engaging CAT/CAPE, where agent evaluation support should land.

signals.title: Customer-Donated Evals Playbook Alignment Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread: Customer donated evals added Playbook - moderate back-and-forth. How customer-donated evals should be captured and tracked, clarification on EH engagement tracking and advisory ownership, playbook alignment across teams.

signals.title: Weekly Executive Status Update Manual Assembly
signals.rawExcerpt: Regular weekly/exec/customer updates follow a very consistent pattern (what changed, risks, blockers, asks), but are recreated manually by different people. The structure is already standardized in practice - only the generation is not.

signals.title: Signal-to-Sync-to-Artifact Repeating Workflow
signals.rawExcerpt: A very consistent Signal to Sync to Artifact loop: (1) Receive signal from email thread raising a blocker, (2) Create a focused meeting or 1:1 e.g. Eval Sync and Chat, (3) Capture outcomes in a durable Loop artifact with decisions, open questions, follow-ups. This three-step pattern shows up repeatedly.

signals.title: Weekly Team Standup (US Pod)
signals.rawExcerpt: US Pod Stand Up - Tuesdays, 12:35-1:00 PM. Core CAPE leadership + pod members. Stable attendee list week over week. Classic same-people, same-time meeting.

signals.title: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Meeting - Mondays, 8:05-8:30 AM. Same v-team, short cadence, clearly a weekly execution sync. Deep technical and evaluation-focused working session covering research deep dives, metrics, evaluation frameworks.

signals.title: Camp AIR Daily Standup
signals.rawExcerpt: [CAPE-US-March] Camp AIR: Standup - Mon-Thu, 1:30-2:00 PM during program window. Same cohort and organizers each time. Program-bounded but very regular while active.

signals.title: CAPE Architecture Call (Weekly)
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Architecture Call - Mondays, 9:05-10:00 AM. Runs weekly with same required distribution. Topics: declarative vs custom agents, convergence roadmap, tooling, orchestration.

signals.title: CAPECAT PM Review (Evals)
signals.rawExcerpt: MCS Analytics and Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review - Wednesdays, 9:00-9:30 AM. Stable PM and leadership set; clearly a standing weekly review.

signals.title: CAPE and Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync - Fridays, 10:00-11:00 AM. Same cross-functional group, same slot every Friday.

signals.title: Learning and Scaling Fridays (Protected Block)
signals.rawExcerpt: Learning and Scaling Fridays PST - Fridays, 2:05-5:00 PM. Organized by manager; same small required group. Long, protected weekly block.

signals.title: The Friday Show (Weekly All-Hands Forum)
signals.rawExcerpt: The Friday Show! - Weekly (Fridays). Magazine-style agenda: National Day highlights, program updates (CAPE Compete V Team), platform updates (Copilot/OpenAI SDK enablement), customer story spotlights and demos. Large, open-attendance forum.

signals.title: External Customer Eval and Demo Sessions
signals.rawExcerpt: External meetings: ~3h 30m total. Includes customer project closure meetings, external office hours with customers or partners. ESS eval planning and Amex evaluation demos cited.

signals.title: Meeting Transcript to Summary and Deck Packaging
signals.rawExcerpt: After meetings, people independently share decks, re-package content for different audiences (ROB decks, exec briefings, customer follow-ups). Sprint demos and builder meetings explicitly call out send deck and automating ROB deck. Same transformation: meeting to audience-specific artifact.

signals.title: Agent Evaluation Framework Guidance (Recurring Asks)
signals.rawExcerpt: You are repeatedly asked to explain eval-driven agent development, gaps in current eval practices, how evals fit before and between MVP stages. Shared agent eval scenario libraries, templates, CSV eval sets, and trajectory/stepwise evaluation concepts. Reviewed large batches of eval results from teammates and customers.

signals.title: Agent Architecture and Identity Explanation (Repeated Questions)
signals.rawExcerpt: People frequently ask to clarify how things actually work under the hood. Common patterns: Do agents have identities/IDs? How do Entra Agent IDs work? Which agents show up in the registry? Signal: seen as someone who can translate ambiguous platform behavior into a coherent mental model.

signals.title: Internal Navigation and Routing Requests
signals.rawExcerpt: A steady stream of requests about navigation: finding recordings, documentation, or approvals; getting permissions or access unblocked; knowing who to loop in, tag, or escalate to. Signal: people see you as someone who understands how the org actually works.

signals.title: Sharing Technical Artifacts (GitHub, SharePoint, Loop, ZIPs)
signals.rawExcerpt: A very large portion of messages contain direct links to working artifacts: GitHub repositories, SharePoint files (pitch decks, CSVs, PPTs, ZIPs), Loop pages for live collaboration, Aka.ms short links. Usually shared as context or building blocks, not just FYIs.

signals.title: EvalCon Content Preparation and Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: EvalCon shows up repeatedly as a deadline-driven milestone and forcing function for clarifying blockers, challenges, and process gaps. You are being pulled into content ownership and framing, not just attendance.

signals.title: Skills Discovery and Agentic Workflow Automation Discussions
signals.rawExcerpt: Another recurring theme is skills as reusable abstractions. Discussed or shared: skill detectors, skill creators, skill libraries; how agents can mine Work IQ (emails, chats, meetings) to detect repeated patterns; turning repeated human workflows into skills.

signals.title: PowerPoint Deck Review and Circulation
signals.rawExcerpt: Decks appear repeatedly: Agents and Platform MPR Mar Working Deck, Builder PM RoB March 18, Monthly Engineering Review, Copilot Cowork Pitch Deck, hackathon prep decks. Regularly reviewing and circulating decision-making, review, and exec-facing decks.

signals.title: Weekly Status Report Loop Page Updates
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple weekly status report Loop files associated with workstreams, updated on a weekly basis. Example: 3/13/2026 Weekly status report CAPE Compete Agent (Loop file) part of a recurring weekly reporting pattern used across teams.

signals.title: Cycle-Based Dashboard Authoring (Skilluminator)
signals.rawExcerpt: Skilluminator Cycle 3 Dashboard (dashboard.html): authored and last modified by you, generated on a weekly cycle (Week of 2026-03-16), explicitly references cycle-based (recurring) analysis and metrics. Strong indicator of a repeatable, cycle-based dashboard update.

signals.title: Excel and CSV Planning and Eval Analytics
signals.rawExcerpt: Several planning, analytics, and evaluation-oriented spreadsheets: MCS Customer Signals for Planning, M365Licenses.csv, eval-related templates. Programmatic, planning, or analysis artifacts consistent with PM work around evals, customer signals, and prioritization.

signals.title: Loop Component Live PM Collaboration
signals.rawExcerpt: Loop files appear multiple times including PM review notes, planning and preview coordination docs. Using Loop as a live collaboration and review surface, especially for meetings, decisions, and ongoing PM threads rather than static docs.

signals.title: Skills and Prototype Artifact Creation (ZIP, HTML, MD)
signals.rawExcerpt: You also touched ZIP packages (skill-detector artifacts), Markdown files, and an HTML dashboard authored and shared. Product-adjacent or experimentation artifacts tied to tooling, skill detection, or internal prototypes.

signals.title: PRD and Spec Creation from Scratch (Duplicated Across Teams)
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple teams independently generate PRDs, design docs, and task plans, often using similar sections (goals, non-goals, risks, KPIs, alternatives). People use different tools and formats for the same conceptual artifact. Camp AIR standups repeatedly describe teams independently creating PRDs.

signals.title: Daily Planning and Day-Prep Page Creation
signals.rawExcerpt: Personal or semi-private planning pages that triage inbox, frame decision lenses, and guide how you engage meetings that day. This shows a workflow of: Prepare to Engage to Capture to Re-prioritize.
